applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused kernel with two-pass GroupNorm
applied_optimizations.description: Initial Triton kernel implementing fused Swish -> GroupNorm -> HardSwish with BLOCK_SIZE=1024

applied_optimizations.name: Increased BLOCK_SIZE to 2048
applied_optimizations.description: Larger block size for better memory coalescing on AMD MI355X

applied_optimizations.name: Increased BLOCK_SIZE to 4096
applied_optimizations.description: Further increased block size for better occupancy

applied_optimizations.name: Precomputed stride optimization
applied_optimizations.description: Precompute strides (stride_n, stride_c, stride_d, stride_h) as compile-time constants to reduce index calculation overhead in the inner loop

applied_optimizations.name: Increased BLOCK_SIZE to 8192
applied_optimizations.description: Maximum practical block size for this problem size (elements_per_group=491148)

attempted_but_failed.name: Using tl.exp instead of tl.sigmoid for Swish

notes: ConvTranspose3d dominates at 56% of runtime - this is highly optimized MIOpen code
Triton kernel is now at ~14ms, down from ~20ms for separate PyTorch ops (GroupNorm + Swish + HardSwish)
Further optimization limited by two-pass nature of GroupNorm (must read data twice)
BLOCK_SIZE=8192 with precomputed strides gives best performance
